entries.text: Earth has land and water. The big areas of water are oceans. The land is split into continents. There are seven continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, and Oceania.
A mountain is a very tall piece of land. Some have snowy tops. The highest mountain on Earth is Mount Everest. People climb mountains with special gear. Hills are smaller than mountains.
A river is running water that flows from high land to lower land, often to the sea or a lake. Rivers give water to plants and animals. People use rivers for drinking water and sometimes for boats.

The world is divided into countries. Each country has its own name, flag, and often its own language. Big towns where lots of people live and work are called cities. You might live in a city, a town, or the countryside.
A map is a drawing of a place. It shows where things are: roads, rivers, countries, or towns. North is often at the top. Maps help us find our way and learn about the world.
question_hints: How do we use a map

entries.text: Different places have different weather. Places near the middle of Earth are often hot. Places near the top or bottom are often cold. Near the sea it might be wet; inland it might be dry. That is why we have deserts, rainforests, and snow.
question_hints: Why is it hot in some places
